mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS over trailing months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present value of stocks and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window in the walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an emerging labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 55% of triggered UNRATE-rise instances across the historical sample, or Rank IC < 0.02 conditional on trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales growth) and HOUST (housing starts growth) jointly indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that is not captured by either series alone, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-HOUST acceleration signal fails to outperform either single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC, or joint Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS elevated), positive INDPRO growth signals resilient industrial output that offsets rate-driven valuation compression, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; the same INDPRO signal is neutral or negative outside tightening regimes.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows no significant directional difference in forward 60d SPX returns between tightening (FEDFUNDS > 4%) and easing regimes, or Rank IC in tightening regime < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rate exceeds 4%, markets price in expectations of aggressive Fed tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as short-term repricing dominates.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns show no significant negative mean (t-test p > 0.05) conditional on CPI YoY exceeding 4% across the historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of decelerating PAYEMS growth and simultaneously rising UNRATE constitutes a compound labor-market weakening signal stronger than either metric alone, since payroll deceleration confirms unemployment increases are structural rather than noise, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS-deceleration and UNRATE-rise signal fails to produce Rank IC above 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or does not outperform UNRATE-only baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive trend in HOUST reflects forward-looking builder confidence and household formation, functioning as a leading indicator of consumption and investment demand that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST trend and forward 5d SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle regime where FEDFUNDS is elevated (above 4%) and GDPC1 growth is simultaneously decelerating, the combination signals restrictive policy colliding with slowing real output, raising recession odds and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; outside this joint condition the signal has no predictive power.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4% AND GDPC1 QoQ growth decelerating for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when both FEDFUNDS > 4% and GDPC1 growth deceleration conditions are jointly triggered, across the historical sample.
